excerpt:
The Fed has hiked rates by 500 bps over the last 14 months, the fastest rate hiking cycle since the early 1980s.

Is this the end of easy money?

Doing a live show w/ @ycharts this week discussing this topic and much more.

Register here, open to all: https://

U.S. employers added 253,000 more workers to nonfarm payrolls in April. Job growth for the prior two months was revised for a net downward revision of 149,000 https://t.co/IDaCThfgDe https://t.co/qFlVPn6S8S

The U.S. labor force participation rate was little changed at 62.6% in April from March. For participation rates by race, sex, educational attainment and other categories, see FRED: https://t.co/Wss5Lsg9TF https://t.co/VU4J67y0dP
Average interest rates for fixed-rate mortgages diverge in the week ended April 27: the 30-year rose to 6.43% while the 15-year fell to 5.71% https://t.co/lYHScSjJWl https://t.co/mPb0LpN1TK
The FRED Blog looks at 30-year fixed-rate average mortgage rates for mortgage applications and traces the interest load for current mortgages https://t.co/xdgGHZT2J0 https://t.co/hifdPaYlm4

May 21 is National #Waiters and #Waitresses Day. 🍽️ 

Seasonally adjusted sales in the Food Services and Drinking Places industry were $81.3 billion in May 2022, up 16.0% from May 2021.

Check out more #CensusEconData: https://t.co/4dNNPmuiX8 https://t.co/SbyS
Apple's 7.4% weighting in the S&P 500 is the largest for any single company in the index with data going back to 1980. $AAPL https://t.co/hFUhbkXUlo

It took 10 rate hikes and 500 bps in cumulative increases, but the Effective Fed Funds Rate (5.08%) is finally above the rate of inflation in the US (4.99%). Since 2010, this has been a rarity, with ultra-easy monetary policy persisting nearly the entire time.
The top 2 stocks in the S&P 500 (Apple & Microsoft) now represent a combined 14.1% of the index, the highest weighting for any two companies with data going back to 1980. https://t.co/a2hTusXX0S
